name: Asparagus and Mushrooms with Black Bean Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1/4 lb brown button mushrooms (or mixed mushrooms)
1 tablespoon sake or other rice wine
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut asparagus on the diagonal into 1-2 inch pieces.
2. Blanch for 5 minutes in boiling water.
3. Drain and set aside.
4. Wash and slice mushrooms. Set aside.
5. Mince the garlic.
6. Rinse the black beans well. Mix garlic and beans together. Set aside.
7. Mix together the rice vinegar, tamari, starch, sugar, and sake. Set aside.
8. Fill a glass with water and set handy next to the stove.
9. Put a skillet over high heat.
10. When the pan is hot, dump the garlic mixture into the pan.
11. Stir fry for one minute, adding water as needed to prevent sticking.
12. Add the asparagus and stir fry for 10 minutes (or until just shy of being crisp-done), adding more water as needed.
13. Add mushrooms and stir fry for 2-5 more minutes.
14. Add the vinegar mixture, stirring well to coat evenly and allowing sauce to thicken.
15. Serve immediately.
Comments:
Fermented black soybeans are available in Asian markets. Usually labeled "salted black beans". I find them in the preserved food section in plastic bags.
